title: Ikari Hebrew naming coincidence
wikitext:
[]: "Ikari" Hebrew naming coincidence?             Quote Post by Aether » Mon Mar 30, 2015 6:18 pm So, has anyone else noticed that the main character, Shinji Ikari's, surname, from Hebrew, literally translates to "main"(עיקרי)? Just me? Ok. Do you think this was done intentionally? Given how many Judeo-Christian mystic symbols they scattered about the show that they didn't seem to do any real research into their actual respective meanings, I'd guess it's just a lucky coincidence, but what do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 30, 2015 6:35 pm Ikari can mean anchor in Japanese, which fits in nicely with many other characters being named after ships or parts of ships.

[]:             Quote Post by Aether » Mon Mar 30, 2015 6:41 pm Yeah I know how nearly all the characters are named after something navy-related... for one reason or another. I've also heard a theory that, spelled differently, it means something to the effect of angry in Japanese. I'm just wondering if anyone else has noticed that it actually has some meaning in hebrew, whether they meant it to or not.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Mar 30, 2015 6:49 pm Maybe they stumbled over it, when they decided to slap Hebrew names onto the Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Aether » Mon Mar 30, 2015 7:08 pm Maybe, Ikari isn't really a Hebrew name, even though it can be a word. Also, did anyone else notice how those "Hebrew" names were mostly heavily distorted, and in some cases I cannot find any non-Eva results for those names.(i.e., Zeruel originally Tzuriel, Amrisael originally wtf idk?)

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Mar 30, 2015 7:12 pm I guess this might have been a fault in the romanisation process on Gainax's front.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 30, 2015 7:42 pm Aether wrote: in some cases I cannot find any non-Eva results for those names.(i.e., Zeruel originally Tzuriel, Amrisael originally wtf idk?)

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Mar 30, 2015 7:43 pm NemZ wrote: Ikari can mean anchor in Japanese, which fits in nicely with many other characters being named after ships or parts of ships.
